REQ-001: When at least N seats are free the service shall grant a hold for N seats with a unique id and a TTL
When fewer than N seats are free the service shall reject the hold for N seats
The confirmed plus currently-held seats shall never exceed the venue capacity (verified=kani)
REQ-002: While a hold is Held the system shall allow exactly one of confirm, release, or expire
When a hold has reached a terminal state the system shall reject every further lifecycle event (verified=tla)
REQ-003: When a client confirms a live hold by id the system shall book its seats permanently
When a client confirms an expired, unknown, or already-confirmed hold the system shall reject the confirmation
REQ-004: When a client releases an unconfirmed hold by id the system shall return its seats to available
When a client releases an unknown or already-expired hold the system shall treat it as a no-op (verified=proptest)
REQ-005: When a hold is not confirmed within its TTL the system shall free its seats automatically
While a hold is within its TTL the system shall keep its seats reserved (verified=proptest)
REQ-006: When a client queries availability the system shall report capacity minus confirmed and currently-held seats
REQ-007: When multiple clients race for the last seats the system shall grant at most the seats that remain (verified=dst)
The service shall serialize every operation so the no-overbooking invariant holds under concurrency
